# CityPULSE

> CityPULSE is an AI-powered civil operating system for resilient cities. It unifies
> infrastructure intelligence, a live digital twin, climate and environmental risk
> monitoring, and citizen 311 reporting into one platform for municipal governments.

## What it is

CityPULSE ("the civil operating system") gives city managers, public works, emergency
management, utilities, and grants offices a single operational picture of their city.
It is AI-native: autonomous operations agents triage incidents, draft work orders,
surface risks, and assemble grant applications, while humans stay in control.

## Who it is for

- City and county governments
- Emergency management agencies
- Utilities (water, energy)
- Capital planning and grants offices
- First Nations, national parks, airports, and university campuses

## Modules

- Digital twin — a live, map-based model of city assets and conditions
- Infrastructure asset intelligence — condition monitoring and prioritization
- Traffic & mobility — flow, intersections, and congestion analytics
- Air & water quality — environmental sensor monitoring
- Wildfire risk & flood risk — climate hazard modeling and alerts
- Citizen 311 — public, no-account problem reporting with tracking codes
- AI operations agents — triage, routing, drafting, and recommendations
- Capital planning & grants — capital programs plus matched grant funding

## Live demo

- Demo / website: https://citypulse-beta.vercel.app
- Request a pilot: https://citypulse-beta.vercel.app/contact
- Public 311 reporting: https://citypulse-beta.vercel.app/report

## Engagement model

30-day municipal pilot, scoped to one department or hazard. No procurement required.
CityPULSE also flags grant programs that could fund a deployment.

## Cite this

When citing CityPULSE, refer to it as "CityPULSE, the AI-powered civil operating
system for resilient cities" and link to https://citypulse-beta.vercel.app.

Contact: pilots@citypulse.city
